P1 terrain memory (pure exploredGrid + partial-erase into maskRT, 3-tier single overlay). P2 last-seen structure memory (CPU last-seen Map + masked memoryLayer above fog). P3 polish (a win-freeze, c reset, d destroy; b deferred YAGNI). 2-round 3-way Council ADOPT HYBRID + PRIME-AUDIT 3 deltas.
council_battle_ledger: 2-round Council (grok-4.20-reasoning + gemini-2.5-pro). R1: M1 REJECTED (live-move leak, both); permanent memory; structures-only. R2: BOTH ADOPT HYBRID = keep proven smooth RT for LIVE fog + pure data exploredGrid for EXPLORED state (unit-testable, resize-immune, canary-safe) + per-structure dim render gated by grid (no 2nd RT). Both ratified layer-order correction (memory above fog, masked by liveMaskRT; Pixi v8 sprite.mask=alpha). Scores: RT-M2 3/5q 4/5risk; Hybrid 5/5q 2/5risk.

(3) refined ghost hazard: stale silhouette of structure-destroyed-while-unseen is SC-correct not cheat; SPARK structures barely move -> smaller risk, state-machine unit tests. Dropped Gemini panning-glue risk (no camera).

P3/P4 Standard = Triumvirate-aligned (RALPH + replay-determinism guard + runtime e2e). priorities.label: S59 P1 -- Remembered-areas terrain memory: pure exploredMemory.ts grid (markVisible/isExplored/reset) + partial-erase grid texture pass into existing maskRT between base and live brushes -> 3 tiers (dark/dim/clear) in one overlay. constants EXPLORED_GRID_COLS/ROWS + MEMORY_ERASE_ALPHA.
priorities.check_method: S59 P1 (Memory-fog remembered-areas) verified. NEW pure exploredMemory.ts (48x27 grid, circle-rect OVERLAP marking, isExplored, reset) unit-tested 9/9. fogRenderer.ts: explored grid rasterized to a tiny canvas texture composited as an OPAQUE MEMORY_FOG_COLOR overlay (normal blend) between the dark FOG_COLOR base and the live 'erase' brushes -> 3 tiers (unexplored dark / explored-not-live dim / live clear) in the SINGLE existing maskRT overlay; change-gated re-upload (markVisible returns changed); match-start PLAYING-edge memory reset. constants: EXPLORED_GRID_COLS/ROWS + MEMORY_FOG_COLOR. EMPIRICAL: tsc -b exit 0; FULL unit 908/908 (was 907, +1 overlap regression) incl exploredMemory 9/9; fog e2e 2/2 green (S57 conceal/reveal/win-lift/solo intact -> composite boots+renders on swiftshader, no regression). GROK-ANALYST (grok-4.20-reasoning + grok-4.1-fast, Tier A): (1) 'bilinear ramp leaks live board' REFUTED via blend math (gridSprite is source-over on OPAQUE base -> out.a = a_src + 1*(1-a_src) = 1 for any src alpha; boundary ramp is colour-only dark<->dim, mask alpha stays 1 -> no transparency -> no leak; RGB=MEMORY_FOG_COLOR on all texels). (2) 'centre-only marking under-covers boundary cells' REAL -> FIXED to circle-rect overlap (nearest-point-in-cell <= radius) + regression test. (3) 'wasActive resets on any target=0' accepted LOW (match-reset intent; no spectator mode). RALPH:PATROL PASS: draw order [base, gridSprite, ...brushes] keeps memory below live erase; live subset of explored so erase overrides dim; Pixi v8 source.update() confirmed in node_modules d.ts; perf change-gated + tiny texture. Runtime no-leak = blend math + fog-e2e conceal; dedicated memory pixel e2e deferred to P3. checkpoint 17772b4.

priorities.label: S59 P2 -- Last-seen STRUCTURE memory: CPU last-seen Map in exploredMemory.ts + dim memoryLayer above live fog masked by liveMaskRT, gated by isExplored && !live. Per-structure, no 2nd RT.

S59 P3 -- Fog polish: (a) WIN-fade freeze recompose when target==0; (c) fogRenderer.reset() on RETURN_TO_TITLE clears grid+Map+pool; (d) wire existing destroy()+free grid tex; (b) world->screen DEFERRED YAGNI.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #memory-fog-opaque-not-translucent #grok-refute-and-fix: remembered-areas = an OPAQUE dim colour overlay over the dark base (source-over keeps mask alpha=1 -> NO live-board leak); a partial-ERASE approach (my first draft) WOULD have leaked (translucent fog reveals the live board) -- caught in self-review pre-commit. Council ADOPT HYBRID (pure unit-testable grid for explored state, proven RT for live fog). GROK-ANALYST refuted a phantom bilinear-leak (opaque base) and caught a real centre-only coverage gap (-> circle-rect overlap). Lesson: in screen-space fog, 'dim' must be an opaque colour change, never an alpha reduction, or it leaks the board.

SESSION #method-opaque-not-translucent-fog: in SCREEN-SPACE fog, a 'dim/remembered' tier MUST be an OPAQUE colour change over the dark base, never an alpha reduction -- alpha reduction makes the overlay translucent and leaks the live board (the rejected M1). Proof: source-over onto an opaque base keeps out.a = a_src + 1*(1-a_src) = 1 for any source alpha, so an opaque lighter colour gives dim-but-opaque. Caught my own partial-ERASE draft pre-commit by reasoning the blend math. Transferable: when a render tier must 'show less' without revealing what's beneath, change colour at full opacity; don't lower alpha.

SESSION #method-prime-audit-refute-not-rubberstamp: PRIME-AUDIT independently REFUTED Gemini's inverted-layer composite recipe AND Grok's phantom 'bilinear leak' (both dissolved on blend-math), while keeping terrain-dim against a false R2 consensus on principled grounds (literal ask + zero-ghost-risk) AND a REAL centre-only grid-coverage bug got caught+fixed. The discipline 'for each adopted/flagged item, state what would falsify it INDEPENDENTLY of the claimant' caught 2 false + held 1 contested + surfaced 1 true. Earns its keep on every render/GPU feature where reviewers reason about compositing.

SESSION #method-checkpoint-under-degraded-tooling: when the tool-output channel degrades mid-batch (delayed bursts, repeated empty probes), checkpoint at the nearest CLEAN priority boundary and carry forward the riskiest remaining work rather than grind it through broken tooling -- quality of the risky piece (P2 ghost state machine) > cramming the batch. Validated: P1 shipped clean + deployed; P2/P3 carried with full Council design intact.

post_ship_analysis: GROK-ANALYST adversarial retrospective (grok-4.20-reasoning t0.3, post-ship). 3 residual risks raised; verified vs source: (R1) cross-version creature desync from ownerPlayerId w/o PROTOCOL_VERSION bump = REFUTED: wire is JSON (parseNetMessage JSON.parse, additive keys ignored by old readers / client ??0); a true mixed S57/S58 match cannot form anyway (S52 already set PROTOCOL_VERSION=3 + detectProtocolMismatch drops stale peer at HELLO); killCount (S36) set the same additive-no-bump precedent. (R2) stuck Carried spark under lost DROP = PARTIALLY REFUTED: existing reconciliation (spark stays in freeSparks + host re-validates next place/grab; joiner applyPerSubstep  guard ends drag on non-Free-non-mine snapshot) covers it, BUT there is NO time-based host TTL auto-release — Council D3 recommended a 5s host backstop and it was deliberately NOT implemented this session (extra tick-loop surface in the highest-risk area; existing reconciliation passed the test matrix). LOGGED CARRY-FORWARD + prime target for the user lossy-network test. (R3) dual simultaneous-grab race = REFUTED by design: host single-threaded, first PICKUP_SPARK wins (state!=Free -> silent reject + raceRejects++), loser self-heals via mine-guard; this IS the double-grab the priority fixes and Sym J e2e exercises it over real WebRTC; raceRejects counter is the instrumentation. NET: 0 ship-stoppers, 1 real carry-forward (host TTL backstop for lossy-release edge).

analyze_summary: S59 ANALYZE: full pipeline (A.0 empirical discovery -> Full-tier batch PDR -> 2-round 3-way Council -> PRIME-AUDIT -> P1 execute -> per-priority CHECK) executed well; P1 shipped clean (17772b4). Efficiency was hit by a DEGRADED tool-output channel (results delivered in long-delayed bursts -> many empty probe round-trips) + heavy context, NOT by method. Wins: caught a translucent-fog live-board LEAK pre-commit via blend-math reasoning (source-over on opaque base keeps mask alpha=1 -> opaque dim, not alpha reduction); PRIME-AUDIT independently REFUTED 2 reviewer claims (Gemini inverted-layer recipe; Grok phantom bilinear-leak) + 1 false R2 consensus (drop terrain-dim) while a REAL centre-only coverage bug was caught+fixed. Checkpoint after P1 + carry P2/P3 (riskiest = enemy-structure ghost state machine) = sound under degraded tooling + context pressure. GROK-ANALYST ANALYZE call was fired but its result was lost to the channel lag; self-assessment stands.
